description: Game modes in The Division 2 with build recommendations, enemy tiers, key mechanics, and tips for each mode.

challenging.name: Challenging
key_mechanics: Enemies have increased health and damage compared to normal/hard
Elite enemies appear more frequently
Checkpoint save system allows mid-mission recovery
Gear Score 490+ recommended
build_priorities: Solid DPS with at least moderate armor
CHC/CHD stacking to around 60/100
Armor on kill for sustain
No specialized role requirements — solo DPS is viable
tips: Challenging is a good entry point for testing new DPS builds before pushing to Heroic.
Enemies deal meaningful damage — don't neglect armor entirely.
A single healer or skill build player can significantly ease group play.
Learning enemy layouts here prepares you for Heroic.

heroic.name: Heroic
key_mechanics: Enemies have very high health pools and deal serious damage
Elites appear in most encounters
No checkpoint saves — dying restarts the encounter
Gear Score 500 strongly recommended
Modifiers (weekly heroic modifiers) can dramatically alter enemy behavior
build_priorities: Maximize weapon damage (Obliterate + Fox's Prayer + CHC/CHD cap)
Damage to Elites is high value — most targets are elites
Armor on kill for sustain between covers
At least one healer recommended for group play
Skill builds remain viable if at Tier 6
tips: Always check the weekly heroic modifiers before running — some modifiers drastically change strategy.
Prioritize elite enemies first; they deal the most damage and can stagger you out of cover.
Cover discipline is critical — Heroic punishes players who stand in the open.
Use Fox's Prayer to punish elites when they break cover or rush your position.
DtE (Damage to Elites) minor attribute provides a large multiplicative bonus here.

legendary.name: Legendary
legendary.enemy_tier: Gold (Named) enemies in large numbers, specialized enemy roles
key_mechanics: Enemies deal massive damage and have enormous health pools
Specialized enemy roles: rushers, snipers, tanks, drone operators, grenadiers
No respawns — all 4 players must be revived by teammates or use a Revive Hive
Loot Bounty system: enemies drop better loot the longer the mission takes
Some missions have unique mechanics such as enemy reinforcement timers
Gear Score 500 mandatory

recommended_roles.solo: Not recommended — technically possible with specific builds but very slow

build_priorities: Revive Hive or Future Initiative healer strongly recommended
At least one tank or high-armor player to hold flanks
Maximum DPS to reduce time exposed to fire
DtE mandatory — nearly every enemy is elite tier
Skill builds with assault turret + striker drone remain strong
Team coordination: assign roles explicitly before starting
tips: Run the mission on Heroic first to learn enemy positions and spawn timers.
Designate a healer — Future Initiative 4pc is the gold standard.
Rushers will close in quickly; have your team assign one player to deal with them.
Never all push at once — at least one player should hold back to revive downed allies.
True Patriot 4pc on one DPS player amplifies the entire team's damage significantly on bosses.
Revive Hive as a second skill slot can save a mission if the healer goes down.
Communication about cover-to-cover movements prevents getting isolated and downed.
Drone snipers in legendary deal extreme damage — clear them with a skill or targeted fire immediately.

raid_dark_hours.name: Operation Dark Hours (Raid)
raid_dark_hours.enemy_tier: Raid-specific elite tiers with unique mechanics
key_mechanics: 8-player cooperative raid with multiple encounter phases
Encounters: Boomer, Warhound, Pappy, Jefferson Trade Center final boss
Each encounter requires specific role assignments and mechanic execution
No matchmaking — must organize a pre-made group of 8
Unique Raid-exclusive loot drops
Phase-based boss fights with callout mechanics (e.g., Warhound armor plates, ISAC network)
build_priorities: Negotiator's Dilemma and True Patriot provide massive group DPS amplification on bosses
At least one Future Initiative healer mandatory
One Foundry Bulwark tank for encounter-specific mechanics
All DPS players should be at maximum gear score with optimized builds
Revive Hive as second skill slot recommended for all non-healer players
tips: Use Discord or voice communication — calling out mechanics is required.
Boomer encounter: split 4v4, each side must take down Boomer's corresponding generators simultaneously.
Warhound: designate who removes each armor plate phase and when to push DPS.
Jefferson final boss: ISAC mechanic requires two players to interface with terminals simultaneously.
Bring Revive Hive on multiple players — being able to self-revive during mechanic phases saves wipes.
Practice each encounter in Normal difficulty before attempting Hard mode.
Negotiate ND marks so all 8 players are shooting the same target.

raid_iron_horse.name: Operation Iron Horse (Raid)
raid_iron_horse.enemy_tier: Raid-specific elite tiers with unique mechanics
key_mechanics: 8-player cooperative raid set in a factory/industrial environment
Encounters: Dogs, Assembly Line boss, Metal Back boss
Assembly Line: conveyor belt mechanics requiring side coordination
Metal Back: rotating phases requiring tank swaps and DPS windows
True Sons faction enemies with heavy armor focus
Unique Iron Horse Raid-exclusive gear drops
build_priorities: Damage to Armor is particularly high value — True Sons enemies use heavy armor
Tank required for Metal Back boss phase swaps
Healer mandatory
High burst DPS for Assembly Line DPS window phases
All players at GS500 with optimized builds
tips: Assembly Line: assign left and right sides clearly. Both sides must complete objectives simultaneously.
Metal Back: the tank must taunt aggro during phase transitions to prevent random players from being targeted.
Dogs encounter: AOE awareness — dogs can quickly swarm and down isolated players.
Damage to Armor minor attribute performs extremely well in this raid due to True Sons composition.
Metal Back DPS window is brief — ensure all DPS players are positioned and ready before triggering.
Healer should track which players are taking tank taunt damage in Metal Back phases.

countdown.name: Countdown
countdown.enemy_tier: Mixed, scales with ongoing round count
key_mechanics: Two teams of 4 complete separate objectives simultaneously before a countdown timer expires
Teams can rendezvous at extraction for the final phase
Rounds get progressively harder as the team advances
Objectives include: destroy generators, disrupt comms, eliminate HVTs
Failing to complete objectives in time ends the run
Exotic and high-tier loot available at late rounds
build_priorities: Speed and damage output — objectives have timers
Sustain is important due to the continuous nature of the mode
Skill builds perform well due to constant use between objectives
Revive Hive strongly recommended — no waiting for squad to revive you
Memento backpack synergizes well with the kill-heavy pace
tips: Split objectives efficiently — one squad member handles the objective while others clear enemies.
At late rounds, bring your best DPS build — enemy health scales significantly.
Coordinate with the other squad at extraction; timing the final phase together reduces exposure.
Eclipse Protocol CC builds can freeze or poison clusters of enemies near objectives.
Memento stacks build rapidly in Countdown due to the constant kill pace.
Don't neglect the extraction phase — enemies spawn in large numbers when the helicopter arrives.

descent.name: Descent
key_mechanics: Roguelike mode — start with no gear and build loadout from room drops and choices
Each room offers a choice of weapon, gear piece, or talent upgrade
No external gear brought in — builds form organically through descent choices
Boss rooms at regular intervals with unique mechanics
Death ends the current run; progress tracked via Descent score
Unlockable permanent upgrades (Directives, stash bonuses) for future runs

recommended_roles.notes: Roles form dynamically based on what drops. Cannot plan a specific build pre-run.

build_priorities: Adaptability — take the best synergistic gear available, not a preset plan
Prioritize CHC and CHD on early drops to scale into mid-game
Talent synergies: if you get Obliterate early, build around CHC to enable it
Skills can carry in early floors — grab a good turret or drone if skill talents appear
Armor-on-kill is especially valuable in Descent due to no external healing
recommended_builds: N/A — build adapts dynamically to descent loot
tips: Take a weapon that matches the talents you've collected, not the other way around.
Prioritize Obliterate or Glass Cannon early if offered — they provide outsized damage gains.
Armor on kill drops should never be ignored — sustain is your lifeline without a healer.
Boss rooms telegraph attacks — move out of red zones, don't tunnel vision on damage output.
On later floors, enemies can one-shot poorly armored builds — take defensive pieces if offered.
Group play allows role specialization even within the roguelike structure.
Unlock permanent Descent bonuses by completing milestone challenges to improve future runs.

dark_zone.name: Dark Zone (PvPvE)
dark_zone.players: Open world (up to 12 players per DZ instance)
key_mechanics: Three Dark Zone areas: DZ East, DZ West, DZ South
Contaminated loot must be extracted via helicopter — players can go rogue to steal
Going Rogue: hostile to other players; killed rogues drop valuable loot
Normalization: in non-Occupied DZ, player stats are partially normalized to reduce gear gap
Occupied Dark Zone: no normalization, PvP is full-power gear; accessible at certain hours
Landmarks: high-value PvE encounters in the DZ with better loot
Dark Zone XP for gaining DZ levels and cosmetics
build_priorities: Burst damage is prioritized — player fights are short
SMG or shotgun for close-range dominance in interior DZ areas
Balance offensive and defensive stats — pure glass cannon builds die to ambushes
Armor on kill is critical for multi-player rogue fights
Fox's Prayer performs extremely well — DZ players frequently break cover
In Occupied DZ: bring your absolute best full DPS build
In normalized DZ: skill builds and balanced builds perform competitively
tips: Watch extraction zones — rogues frequently camp them waiting to steal your contaminated loot.
Use the DZ map (M) to track other players near you; yellow markers indicate rogues.
Going rogue for contaminated loot can be profitable if your team can win the resulting fight.
In occupied DZ, expect coordinated full-build groups — bring your best gear.
Keener's Watch exotic pistol drops exclusively in the Dark Zone.
Landmarks respawn on a timer — cycle multiple landmarks to maximize loot per DZ session.
If you hear a helicopter call, decide quickly whether to contest it or let it go.

conflict.name: Conflict (PvP)
key_mechanics: Structured PvP with stat normalization — all players stats are equalized to reduce gear gap
Game modes: Skirmish (team deathmatch), Domination (point capture)
Normalization makes build optimization still matter but reduces extreme gaps
Respawn timers increase as match progresses
Conflict-exclusive cosmetics and caches as rewards
build_priorities: Burst damage in SMG or shotgun for close-range encounters
Movement speed and armor are more important than in PvE due to normalization
Cover usage and positioning outweigh raw stats in Conflict
Skill builds are situationally viable — turrets and drones can hold objectives
Revive Hive is useful for supporting teammates in objective modes
tips: Normalization reduces the gear gap significantly — game sense and communication matter most.
In Domination, capturing points earns more points than kills — don't over-pursue kills.
Smokes and flashbangs (Chem Launcher) can deny points or force enemies off objectives.
Learn spawn locations to anticipate enemy approaches.
SMGs dominate interior Conflict maps; rifles and ARs perform better on open maps.
Conflict is a good environment for learning PvP without the ambush risk of the Dark Zone.

summit.name: The Summit
summit.enemy_tier: Scales with floor level and selected difficulty modifier
key_mechanics: 100-floor skyscraper with increasing difficulty
Each floor is a combat encounter with randomized enemy composition
Floor modifiers alter enemy behavior or player conditions
Faction changes every 20-25 floors (True Sons, Black Tusk, Outcasts, Hyenas)
Boss floors appear at regular intervals with unique mechanics
Summit-exclusive loot drops increase in quality with floor level
Can start from any previously reached floor checkpoint
build_priorities: Sustained DPS over burst — encounters chain continuously between floors
Armor on kill is extremely high value — constant kills between floors
Skill builds with low-cooldown skills perform well due to short floor transitions
Healer or Revive Hive important on floors 80-100 at legendary difficulty
Memento backpack shines in Summit due to the constant kill pace
Adapt to faction modifiers — Black Tusk floors require more DtA (Damage to Armor)
tips: Floors 1-40 can be completed casually; floors 80-100 are equivalent to legendary difficulty.
Prioritize clearing floor 100 on each faction rotation for the best loot table.
Armor on kill sustains you between medkits on high floors without a healer.
Black Tusk (floors ~60-80) use drone operators — take them out immediately to avoid drone harassment.
Outcast floors with Pestilence-type enemies can apply poison — stack some hazard protection.
Eclipse Protocol CC builds excel on floors with packed Outcast or Hyena enemy groups.
If playing solo, focus on floors where your faction counter-build is strongest.
The floor modifier UI shows the current modifier before entering — adjust positioning strategy accordingly.

incursion_clear_sky.name: Clear Sky (Incursion)
key_mechanics: Legacy incursion from Division 1 era; available in Division 2 as replayable content
AA gun defense mechanic — players must protect AA emplacements from waves of enemies
Final boss phase with large health pool
Rewards unique incursion caches
build_priorities: Sustained DPS for wave defense phases
Crowd control for managing multiple enemy waves
Healer recommended for final boss phase
Skill builds with turrets can cover multiple AA gun positions simultaneously
tips: Assign each player a specific AA gun to defend during wave phases.
Skill builds with assault turrets can autonomously defend a position during AA waves.
In the final boss phase, avoid being caught in the open — maintain cover discipline.
Eclipse Protocol CC builds can manage multiple enemy clusters near AA emplacements effectively.

incursion_dragons_nest.name: Dragon's Nest (Incursion)
incursion_dragons_nest.enemy_tier: Elite Black Tusk enemies with armored unit focus
key_mechanics: Black Tusk-themed incursion in Division 2
Heavy armored enemy presence — Damage to Armor highly effective
Multi-phase encounter with objective-based progression
Aerial drone threats in later phases
Unique incursion rewards including named items
build_priorities: Damage to Armor minor attribute highly recommended — Black Tusk uses heavy armor extensively
Anti-drone capability — skill builds with Firefly or Seeker Mines effective against drones
Sustained DPS for armor-plated enemies
Healer helps in later phases where drone swarms increase pressure
tips: Stack Damage to Armor on minor attribute slots — Black Tusk armor plates absorb large amounts of damage.
Firefly skill set to Blinder variant can neutralize drone threats before they overwhelm the team.
Use cover aggressively — Black Tusk enemies flank more intelligently than other factions.
In the final phase, prioritize armor-plate enemies over standard enemies to clear the pressure quickly.
Assault Turret at Tier 6 can sustain significant damage against armored Black Tusk units autonomously.
